---
title: "Participant Information Sheet"
---

**Project Title:** Closing the AI Literacy Gap: Scaffolding Equitable Participation in AI-Integrated Assessment

**Investigators:** Michael Borck (PI); Marcela Moraes (Co-I); Torsten Reiners (Co-I)

**Participating Unit:** [Unit Placeholder]

---

## What is this study about?

We are studying how students interact with AI during short tasks and whether a one-line reminder increases critical engagement.

## What will I be asked to do?

- Attend a 45-60 minute lab session in a Curtin computer lab.
- Complete a short, course-relevant task using a university-provided AI tool (no personal accounts).
- You may see a one-line reminder appended to the AI's replies.
- Complete a brief digital exit survey (approximately 10 minutes).

## Are there any risks?

Risks are minimal (e.g., mild performance pressure). You may skip questions or stop at any time. Participation does not affect grades.

## Are there any benefits?

You may improve your AI interaction skills; findings will inform fair AI-integrated assessment at Curtin.

## Privacy and confidentiality

Your student number is collected when you log into the session. This is used only to link equity indicators (such as low SES or first-in-family status) to your session data. Your student number is then permanently deleted. Your conversation, task output, and survey responses are tagged with a random session code only - your name and student number do not appear in the research data. The research team analyses only the de-identified dataset.

## Voluntary participation and withdrawal

Participation is voluntary and will not affect your studies. You may withdraw at any time before data de-identification by contacting the research team and quoting your session code. Once the de-identification process has been completed and the linkage between student numbers and session codes has been destroyed, withdrawal is no longer possible as individual records cannot be identified.

## Contacts

- For study questions: [PI email/phone]
- For ethics concerns: [Curtin HREC email/phone]
